100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 8, 1926, the League of Nations convened in Geneva, addressing ongoing tensions in Europe and discussing disarmament efforts. Meanwhile, the United States continued to grapple with Prohibition, as various states debated the effectiveness of the 18th Amendment. In the world of sports, heavyweight boxing champion Jack Dempsey was in the spotlight, with speculations about a potential title fight looming on the horizon.
